Defines the RotBmpPairLayer structure and functions for managing paired rotated bitmap layers.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines a structure and functions for managing a pair of rotating bitmap layers. The `RotBmpPairLayer` structure contains two `RotBitmapLayer` objects, `white_layer` and `black_layer`, which are used to create an image with distinct white, black, and transparent regions. The file provides function declarations for initializing ([`rotbmp_pair_layer_init`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_init>)) and deinitializing ([`rotbmp_pair_layer_deinit`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_deinit>)) the `RotBmpPairLayer`, as well as functions to set and increment the rotation angle ([`rotbmp_pair_layer_set_angle`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_set_angle>), [`rotbmp_pair_layer_increment_angle`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_increment_angle>)). Additionally, it includes a function to set the source image center ([`rotbmp_pair_layer_set_src_ic`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_set_src_ic>)) and a function to invert the colors of the layers ([`rotbmp_pair_layer_inver_colors`](<#rotbmp_pair_layer_inver_colors>)). The header file includes necessary dependencies for layer and graphics types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/layer.h`
- `applib/ui/rotate_bitmap_layer.h`
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`


# Data Structures

---
### RotBmpPairLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``layer``: A base `Layer` object for the `RotBmpPairLayer`.
    - ``white_layer``: A `RotBitmapLayer` for the white-transparent image.
    - ``black_layer``: A `RotBitmapLayer` for the black-transparent image.
- **Description**: Represents a pair of rotating bitmap layers, one for a white-transparent image and one for a black-transparent image, used to create a composite image with distinct white, black, and transparent regions.

### rotbmp\_pair\_layer\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:rotbmp_pair_layer_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L36>)

Initializes a `RotBmpPairLayer` with two bitmaps.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure with two `GBitmap` objects representing white-transparent and black-transparent images. This function must be called before using the `RotBmpPairLayer` for rendering. The `white` and `black` bitmaps must have identical dimensions; otherwise, the function logs an error and returns without initializing the layer. Ensure that the bitmaps do not overlap each other to maintain the intended visual effect.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure that will be initialized. The caller must allocate this structure before calling the function.
    - `white`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` representing the white-transparent image. Must have the same dimensions as `black`. The caller retains ownership.
    - `black`: A pointer to a `GBitmap` representing the black-transparent image. Must have the same dimensions as `white`. The caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_init`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_init>)  (Implementation)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L38>)

Deinitializes a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure.
- **Description**: Use this function to deinitialize a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure when it is no longer needed. This function releases resources associated with the `RotBmpPairLayer`, including its internal layers. Call this function to prevent memory leaks after you finish using the `RotBmpPairLayer`. Ensure that the `RotBmpPairLayer` has been initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure that must have been previously initialized. The pointer must not be null, and the function does not handle null pointers.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_deinit`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_deinit>)  (Implementation)

### rotbmp\_pair\_layer\_set\_angle<!-- {{#callable_declaration:rotbmp_pair_layer_set_angle}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L40>)

Sets the rotation angle for both layers in a `RotBmpPairLayer`.
- **Description**: Use this function to set the rotation angle for both the white and black layers of a `RotBmpPairLayer`. This function is useful when you need to rotate the composite image formed by the pair of layers. Ensure that the `RotBmpPairLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function sets the angle for both the `white_layer` and `black_layer` within this structure.
    - `angle`: An integer representing the angle in degrees to set for the rotation. The function applies this angle to both layers in the `RotBmpPairLayer`.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_set_angle`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_set_angle>)  (Implementation)

### rotbmp\_pair\_layer\_increment\_angle<!-- {{#callable_declaration:rotbmp_pair_layer_increment_angle}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L41>)

Adjusts the rotation angle of both layers in a `RotBmpPairLayer` by a specified amount.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the rotation angle of both the white and black layers in a `RotBmpPairLayer` by a given increment. This function is useful when you need to animate or adjust the rotation of the image pair dynamically. Ensure that the `RotBmpPairLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function. The function does not validate the input angle change, so it is the caller's responsibility to provide a valid increment.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the rotation angle of the `white_layer` and `black_layer` within this structure.
    - `angle_change`: An integer representing the amount to increment the current rotation angle. There are no restrictions on the value, but the caller should ensure it is appropriate for the application.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_increment_angle`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_increment_angle>)  (Implementation)

### rotbmp\_pair\_layer\_set\_src\_ic<!-- {{#callable_declaration:rotbmp_pair_layer_set_src_ic}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L43>)

Sets the source image center for a rotated bitmap pair layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to define the source image center point for both the white and black layers of a `RotBmpPairLayer`. This function adjusts the source image center for rotation operations, ensuring that both layers share the same center point. It also updates the frame of the main layer to match the bounds of the white layer. Call this function when you need to change the rotation center of the image pair.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the `white_layer` and `black_layer` within this structure.
    - `ic`: A `GPoint` structure representing the new source image center. This point is used for both the white and black layers.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_set_src_ic`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_set_src_ic>)  (Implementation)

### rotbmp\_pair\_layer\_inver\_colors<!-- {{#callable_declaration:rotbmp_pair_layer_inver_colors}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/rotbmp_pair_layer.h#L46>)

Exchanges the black and white layers in a `RotBmpPairLayer`.
- **Description**: Use this function to swap the black and white layers of a `RotBmpPairLayer` object. This operation is useful when you need to invert the color representation of the image pair. Ensure that the `RotBmpPairLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function. The function modifies the internal state of the `RotBmpPairLayer` by exchanging its `black_layer` and `white_layer` components.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pair`: A pointer to a `RotBmpPairLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function swaps the `black_layer` and `white_layer` within this structure.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`rotbmp_pair_layer_inver_colors`](<rotbmp_pair_layer.c.md#rotbmp_pair_layer_inver_colors>)  (Implementation)
